rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical regioselective 1,2-diarylation of alkenes with cyanoarenes and aryl halides via cathodic reduction without transition-metal catalyst
electrochemical_role: Cathodic single-electron reduction of dicyanobenzene (1aa) to its radical anion (DCA), which acts as a redox mediator to reduce aryl halide (ArX) to aryl radical via outer-sphere electron transfer; sacrificial Zn anode is oxidized to Zn(II)

key_intermediates.name: aryl halide radical anion (R2, e.g. iodobenzene radical anion)
benzyl radical adduct (13) from anti-Markovnikov addition of aryl radical to alkene
radical/radical-anion cross-coupling adduct (14) bearing cyano group prior to CN- loss

electron_flow: Cathode reduces dicyanobenzene 1aa to its persistent radical anion (DCA); DCA transfers an electron (OSET) to ArX, which fragments to halide and aryl radical; the aryl radical adds anti-Markovnikov to the styrene to give a benzyl radical, which couples with a second DCA and ejects cyanide to give the 1,2-diarylated product. Zn anode is sacrificially oxidized to Zn(2+).
rate_determining_step: Outer-sphere electron transfer from dicyanobenzene radical anion to aryl iodide (ΔG‡ = 6.0 kcal/mol) followed by aryl radical addition to alkene (ΔG‡ = 8.0 kcal/mol via TS-3); radical addition is likely turnover-limiting in the productive cycle

probe_reagents.name: (1-phenyl-2,2-diphenyl)cyclopropyl alkene (radical clock 8)
probe_reagents.observation: ring-opened diarylation product 9 formed in 56% yield, confirming radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: DMPO (5,5-dimethyl-1-pyrroline N-oxide)
probe_reagents.observation: DMPO-phenyl adduct detected by EPR (g=2.0072, A_N=14.4 G, A_H=20.9 G) and confirmed by HR-MS (190.12202)

notes: Alternative pathway via radical addition of DCA itself to the alkene (TS-1, ΔG‡=33.5 kcal/mol) and subsequent CN- dissociation (TS-2) was computationally ruled out due to steric repulsion and instability of intermediate benzyl radical 11. CV studies show 1aa is easier to reduce than 2aa, and constant-potential experiments at -1.6/-1.7 V (where only 1aa is reducible) still give diarylation, supporting the mediator role of DCA over direct ArX reduction. Final C-C bond formation proceeds via radical/radical-anion cross-coupling between benzyl radical and DCA with loss of CN-.
